label: Mapbox
description: Access Mapbox maps, geocoding, routing, and static image APIs using your account access token.

form.title: Connect Mapbox
form.subtitle: Paste your Mapbox access token to enable maps, geocoding, routing, and static image generation.

methods.label: Access Token
methods.description: Paste a public or secret access token from your Mapbox account. The quickest way to connect — no OAuth setup required.
methods.how_to:
## Find your Mapbox access token

1. Log in at [account.mapbox.com](https://account.mapbox.com).
2. Click **Access Tokens** in the left sidebar (or go directly to [account.mapbox.com/access-tokens](https://account.mapbox.com/access-tokens)).
3. You'll see a **Default public token** starting with `pk.eyJ` — this is sufficient for most map, geocoding, routing, and static image requests.
4. To create a scoped secret token (starts with `sk.eyJ`) with specific permissions, click **+ Create a token**, name it (e.g. `Anton`), select the required scopes, and click **Create token**.
5. Copy the token — secret tokens (`sk.`) are shown only once, so save it somewhere safe before closing the page.
6. Paste the token into the **Access Token** field below.

## Notes

- Public tokens (`pk.`) are safe for read-only map and geocoding calls but are visible in client-side code. Secret tokens (`sk.`) are needed for some server-side APIs (e.g. Uploads, Datasets).
- You can restrict a token to specific URLs or API endpoints under **Allowed URLs** on the token detail page.
- Rotate a compromised token by deleting it in the Mapbox dashboard and creating a new one — then update the value below.

fields.name: access_token
fields.label: Access Token
fields.description: Your Mapbox access token. Starts with pk.eyJ (public) or sk.eyJ (secret). Found at account.mapbox.com → Access Tokens.

fields.name: username
fields.label: Mapbox Username
fields.description: Your Mapbox account username. Required for Datasets, Uploads, and Styles APIs that include the username in the endpoint path.
